title: poa nemoralis subsp. caespitosa
intro: Poa nemoralis subsp. caespitosa, also known as the tufted meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the tufted hair-grass plant (Poa nemoralis) that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Poa nemoralis subsp. caespitosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal preparation methods.
